description: Overview of the fundamental concepts of recovery and lifestyle: the role of rest, fatigue, sleep, nutrition, and hydration in sports performance.

questions.text: What is the role of recovery in sport?
questions.explanation: Recovery is the body's process of returning to its pre-training state and preparing for the next training load — without it, development cannot occur.

options.text: The body's return and preparation for the next training load

questions.text: Sleep is one of the most important tools for recovery.
questions.explanation: True. During sleep, the body repairs muscles, consolidates learning, and regulates the hormonal system — which is why athletes need at least 7–9 hours of sleep.

questions.text: Why is adequate hydration important during training?
questions.explanation: Adequate fluid replacement maintains physical performance capacity, regulates body temperature, and prevents the performance-reducing effects of dehydration.

options.text: To maintain performance capacity and regulate body temperature
To make the training session longer

questions.text: What is the role of nutrition in an athlete's lifestyle?
questions.explanation: Proper nutrition provides the energy needed for training, the nutrients for muscle building and recovery, and supports the body's general health.

options.text: It serves only to reduce body weight
It provides energy and nutrients for training, recovery, and general health
It is only important on competition day

questions.text: Rest days are unnecessary parts of the training plan.
questions.explanation: False. Rest days are necessary for supercompensation to occur; adaptation and development can only take place with adequate rest.

questions.text: Which lifestyle factor influences sports performance?
questions.explanation: Sports performance is shaped by a complex set of lifestyle factors: sleep, nutrition, hydration, stress, and the social environment all affect recovery and development.

options.text: Sleep, nutrition, hydration, stress, and social environment together

questions.text: What are the signs of fatigue after training?
questions.explanation: Fatigue is a natural consequence of training: reduced performance, muscle soreness, and impaired concentration characterise it. This is a normal phenomenon that resolves with rest.

options.text: Reduced performance, muscle soreness, and impaired concentration

questions.text: Post-exercise protein intake supports muscle recovery.
questions.explanation: True. Post-exercise protein intake (e.g., 20–40 g) stimulates muscle protein synthesis, facilitating muscle repair and growth.

questions.text: How many hours of sleep per day are recommended for athletes for optimal recovery?
questions.explanation: Athletes are generally recommended 7–9 hours of sleep, which is slightly more than the standard 7–8 hour adult recommendation — the increased physical demand and muscle synthesis process require more sleep for recovery.

options.text: The amount of sleep does not affect recovery

questions.text: Stress and lifestyle do not affect an athlete's recovery.
questions.explanation: False. Psychological stress, poor sleep, and an inadequate lifestyle slow recovery and reduce the extent of adaptation achieved through training.
